reasoning.typography: Times New Roman default, Arial Black for headlines — zero typographic identity. Pure HTML 4.0 browser defaults with no deliberate type system.
reasoning.hierarchy: Weak visual hierarchy. The logo/header image dominates slightly, but text is uniform size. The nav links blend into body text. No clear entry point after the hero image.
reasoning.distinctiveness: Extremely distinctive through sheer anachronism — this is a time capsule from 1998. The naval ship imagery, technical credentials, and 40-year service claim are specific. Could not swap for another business.
reasoning.color: Blue hyperlinks, some blue headlines, but mostly default black text on white. Not exactly restrained by design — just default HTML. The logo has red/blue/yellow but no cohesive palette.
reasoning.density: Default browser spacing throughout. Some awkward line breaks and centered alignment create uneven density. Not confident — just undesigned. The credentials paragraph is dense while other areas float.
reasoning.voice: The structure is actually authentic — technical certifications, military credentials, specific client types (Navy/Merchant/Work Boats). Not a stencil IA. This is a specialist B2B site showing expertise, not 'Currently/Selected/Testimonials'.

winning_patterns: Absolutely distinctive through unabashed late-90s HTML authenticity
Specific technical credentials (Intellian VSAT/TYRO, SeaTel certifications) immediately establish expertise
Hero image of actual military vessel (USS Independence) demonstrates real-world deployment
40-year service claim prominent and believable given the aesthetic
Direct contact info (phone, fax, physical address) signals serious B2B operation
anti_patterns: HTML 4.0 Transitional DOCTYPE — ancient semantic structure
Center-aligned everything with no grid or layout system
Inline styles and table-based layout remnants
Unoptimized 611px JPG hero with alt text/title duplication
Meta keywords stuffing ('navel electronics, navel antenna, naval antenna, NAVEL')
Zero responsive design considerations despite viewport meta tag
Blue hyperlinks as only color strategy
